volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Merchant (Section 5.4.980 - Section 5.4.1025)

sections.label: Section 5.4.980
sections.original: Quem quidem hercle ego , in exilium cum iret , redduxi domum ;
sections.translation: whom , in fact , upon my faith , I brought back home just when he was setting out in self-banishment ; for he was going into exile .

sections.label: Section 5.4.981b
sections.original: An abiit ?
sections.translation: Has he gone then ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.981c
sections.original: Etiam loquere , larua ?
sections.translation: What , do you speak , you hobgoblin ? At this time of life you ought to abstain from those pursuits .

sections.label: Section 5.4.983
sections.original: Fateor , deliqui profecto .
sections.translation: I confess it ; undoubtedly I ’ ve acted wrong .

sections.label: Section 5.4.983b
sections.original: Etiam loquere , larua ?
sections.translation: What , do you speak , you hobgoblin ? You ought at this time of life to have done with these guilty practices . Just as the seasons of the year , so different lines of conduct befit different ages ;

sections.label: Section 5.4.985
sections.original: nam si istuc ius est , senecta aetate scortari senes ,
sections.translation: but if this is proper , that old fellows should be wenching in their old age , where in the world is our common welfare ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.986b
sections.original: Ei , perii miser .
sections.translation: Alas ! wretch that I am ! I ’ m undone .

sections.label: Section 5.4.987
sections.original: Adulescentes rei agendae isti magis solent operam dare .
sections.translation: The young men are more in the habit of giving their attention to following those pursuits .

sections.label: Section 5.4.988
sections.original: Iam obsecro hercle vobis habete cum porcis , cum fiscina .
sections.translation: Troth , now , prithee , do take her to yourselves , with pigs and with basket [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.4.989
sections.original: Redde illi .
sections.translation: Restore her to your son ;

sections.label: Section 5.4.989b
sections.original: Sibi habeat , iam ut volt per me sibi habeat licet .
sections.translation: So far as I ’ m concerned , he may have her .

sections.label: Section 5.4.990
sections.original: Temperi edepol , quoniam ut aliter facias non est copia .
sections.translation: High time , i ’ faith , since you haven ’ t the power of doing otherwise .

sections.label: Section 5.4.991
sections.original: Supplici sibi sumat quid volt ipse ob hanc iniuriam ,
sections.translation: For this injury let him take what satisfaction he likes ; only do you make peace , I beg of you , that he mayn ’ t be angry with me . I ’ faith , if I had known it , or if , indeed , he had told me in the slightest way of joke that he was in love with her , I should never have proceeded to take her away from him so in love .

sections.label: Section 5.4.995
sections.original: Eutyche , ted oro , sodalis eius es , sérva et subveni :
sections.translation: Enitychus , you are his companion , preserve and rescue me , I beg of you . Make this old fellow your client . You shall say that I ’ m mindful of a kindness .

sections.label: Section 5.4.997
sections.original: Ora ut ignoscat delictis tuis atque adulescentiae .
sections.translation: Entreat him that he ’ ll pardon his offences and his youthful age [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.4.998
sections.original: Pergin tu autem ? heia , superbe invehere . spero ego mihi quoque
sections.translation: Heyday now , are you still persisting in inveighing against me with your airs ? I trust that a like opportunity will befall me as well for returning you a similar compliment .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1000
sections.original: Missas iam égo istas artis feci .
sections.translation: I ’ ve long made an end of those pursuits .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1000b
sections.original: Et quidem ego dehinc iam .
sections.translation: And really so shall I from this time forward .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1000c
sections.original: Nihil agis :
sections.translation: Not a bit of it . Through usage your inclinations will be leading you to it again .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1001b
sections.original: Obsecro ,
sections.translation: Prithee , do now be satisfied . Rather , scourge me with thongs even , if you like .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1003
sections.original: Recte dicis . sed istuc uxor faciet , quom hoc resciverit .
sections.translation: You say right . But that your wife will do , when she comes to know of this .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1004
sections.original: Nihil opust resciscat .
sections.translation: There ’ s no need for her to come to know of it .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1004b
sections.original: Quid istic ? non resciscet , ne time .
sections.translation: What ’ s that ? She shan ’ t come to know of it ; don ’ t be afraid .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1005
sections.original: eamus intro , non utibilest hic locus , factis tuis ,
sections.translation: Let ’ s go in-doors ; this place isn ’ t a suitable one for your practices , for there to be persons to overhear who are passing through the street , while we are talking .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1007
sections.original: Hercle qui tu recte dicis : eadem brevior fabula
sections.translation: Why , faith , you say what ’ s right ; that way the story will be shorter . Let ’ s be off

sections.label: Section 5.4.1008b
sections.original: Hic est intus filius apud nos tuos .
sections.translation: Your son is in-doors here at our house .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1009
sections.original: Optumest . illac per hortum nos domum transibimus .
sections.translation: It ’ s very good . We ’ ll pass that way through the garden [*] home .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1010
sections.original: Eutyche , hanc volo prius rém agi , quam meum intró refero pedem .
sections.translation: Eutychus , I want this affair to be settled before I set my foot again within doors .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1011
sections.original: Quid istuc est ?
sections.translation: What is it ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.1011b
sections.original: Suam quisque homo rem meminit . responde mihi :
sections.translation: Each person thinks about his own concerns . Answer me this : do you know for certain that your mother isn ’ t angry with me ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.1012b
sections.original: Scio .
sections.translation: I do know it .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1013
sections.original: Vide .
sections.translation: Take care .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1013b
sections.original: Mea fide .
sections.translation: Trust me for it . Are you satisfied ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.1013c
sections.original: Satis habeo . sed quaeso hercle , etiam vide .
sections.translation: I am . But still , troth now , prithee , do take care .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1014
sections.original: Non mihi credis ?
sections.translation: Don ’ t you believe me ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.1014b
sections.original: Immo credo , sed tamen metuo miser .
sections.translation: Yes , I do believe you ; but still I ’ m dreadfully afraid .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1015
sections.original: Eamus intro .
sections.translation: Let ’ s go in-doors .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1015b
sections.original: Immo dicamus senibus legem censeo ,
sections.translation: Aye , but I think we must pronounce . the law for the old men before we depart , on what terms they are to keep check upon themselves and to be continent . Whoever shall be sixty years of age , if we know of any one , whether husband or , i ’ faith , whether bachelor , in fact , who goes a wenching , upon these terms shall we deal with him ; we shall deem him a fool .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1020
sections.original: et per nos quidem hercle egebit qui suom prodegerit .
sections.translation: And , i ’ faith , so far as we ’ re concerned , he shall be in want who has squandered away his property . And let no one hereafter forbid his youthful son to be in love and to keep a mistress , so it be done in a decent manner . If he shall forbid him , let him , unknown to himself , suffer more loss than if he had openly permitted him . Let this law , then , from this night forward , be binding upon the old men .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1025
sections.original: bene valete ; atque , adulescentes , haec si vobis lex placet ,
sections.translation: ( To the AUDIENCE .) Young men , kindly fare you well ; and if this law , enacted for the sake of the old ones , pleases you , it is right that you should give us loud applause .
